name: Tasting → Booking Conversion Tracking (2026 + Oct 26 Tasting Detail)
subject: Comeketo tracks tasting-to-booking conversion as the primary throughput metric for the sales team. Two source files: (1) rolling monthly summary showing tasting→booking % for 2026 Jan-April, and (2) per-tasting attendee-level detail (e.g., Oct 26 tasting). This is the metric that validates or invalidates the $2,350 bonus-stack + 2-year-price-lock closing machinery described in the Welcome/Wedding decks.
source_files: MONITORING SHEET(Tastings2025-2026) - 2026 -MONTHLY TASTING CONVERSION RATE SUMMARY.csv

ytd_totals_through_2026-04-20.note: Pending is large relative to won (29 pending vs 49 won YTD). If even half of pending converts, the real seasonal rate lands closer to 50%.

monthly_summary_2026.conversion_stability_observation: The monthly win% is tight — 37% to 41% across Jan/Feb/Mar/Apr. This is either a very consistent sales machine or a ceiling the current pitch can't break through. Given the bonus stack is advertised as a one-time tasting-day offer, the expected pattern would be higher conversion among attendees who get the stack — that's the A/B framework the sales team should run.

october_26_tasting_detail.note_on_file_placement: Filename 'October 26 tasting' indicates per-tasting attendee detail. Placed as 2025-10-26 based on the 2025-2026 monitoring sheet title. Verify with Ops.

attendees.loss_reason_direct_quote: We were not impressed with the taste or quality of the food.

attendees.loss_reason: They decided to use a different caterer.

attendees.notes: Attended the tasting to rebuild his quote — already a won deal.

attendees.loss_reason_direct_quote: Disappointed with the service and the food.

data_quality_issue: Row 5 in the source CSV has Lead Name = 'isabela & Rafael' but the Notes, Quote, Tasting sheet, and QR Code columns all refer to Lisa Gauthier. Corrected to 'Lisa Gauthier' in this index. Flag for source-sheet cleanup.

conversion_for_oct_26.note: Below the 2026 YTD average of 39.5%. Small sample.

andre.note: The Jose Lopes 'win' is a rebuild of an existing deal, not net-new — so functionally Andre went 0-for-2 net-new on this tasting. One of those losses is a direct food/service quality complaint. Feed this into Andre's coaching file.

losses_breakdown.reason: 'Not impressed with taste or quality of the food.'

ren.note: Both of Ren's tasting leads here were 80-guest weddings. One lost to quality, one pending. Also feeds Ren's coaching record.

eduarda.note: Only tasting on this sheet with 'won after follow up' — consistent with Eduarda's strong follow-through pattern from the sales scorecard.

per_owner_signal_from_oct_26.cross_reference: Check against people/andre_raw.json, people/ren.json, people/eduarda.json — this is attendee-level data that the monthly summary doesn't expose.

tasting_day_quality_signal.critical_observation: 2 of 6 tasting losses on this single day cite food/service quality directly:
quotes: "We were not impressed with the taste or quality of the food." — Crystal Brown, LOST
"Disappointed with the service and the food." — Lisa Gauthier, LOST
tasting_day_quality_signal.implication: If quality complaints are a repeating loss driver across tastings, the pitch-side fix (bigger bonus stack, better close) won't move the conversion number. Kitchen/service side is the bottleneck.
tasting_day_quality_signal.action: Pull loss-reason categorization across all 2026 tastings to see if this is a pattern or a one-off bad day. If quality complaints recur in > ~15% of losses, that's a Rodrigo/kitchen conversation, not a sales conversation.

secondary_metrics_to_track_but_not_yet_i: Bonus-stack version redeemed (are we A/B testing the $2,350+ stack?)
Price-lock invocation rate (how many bookings cite the 2-year lock as the deciding factor?)
Loss-reason category (quality / price / competitor / logistics / other) — currently free-text, should become a controlled vocabulary
Days from tasting to decision (fast wins vs slow closes)
Per-owner tasting conversion (scorecard has Owner win%, but not segmented to tasting attendees specifically)
metrics_framework_implications.ceiling_hypothesis: 39-41% stable band suggests the current pitch machinery caps near ~40% under today's quality/price/logistics inputs. Moving the ceiling needs either (a) a quality upgrade (reduces food-complaint losses) or (b) a tighter close on the ~30% MAYBE/pending bucket.

feeds_into: knowledge/sales_team_scorecard_oct_2025.json — per-owner tasting win rate is a finer-grained number than the scorecard's overall 'win rate %'
knowledge/brand_wedding_experience.json + brand_welcome_rodrigo.json — validates whether the $2,350 bonus stack is actually moving conversion
projects/* — a future 'Tasting Conversion Improvement' project, owner Rodrigo/Andre, would live here
needs_from_elsewhere: Full 2025 monthly summary — this file only shows 2026 Jan-April, but the filename says 2025-2026. Did 2025 live in a separate tab?
Per-tasting attendee sheets for every tasting, not just Oct 26 — to run loss-reason analysis

notes: The monthly summary has 20+ #DIV/0! rows — months May 2026 onward that haven't happened yet. Expected, not an error.
Lead Owner field is all-caps (EDUARDA, REN, ANDRE). Match to people/* slugs lowercase.

The 'deal_value_guestx30' column name hints at a pricing rule-of-thumb: estimated deal = guest_count × $30. For Lisa Gauthier's 120-guest fundraiser that would be $3,600; for an 80-guest wedding around $2,400. That's low for weddings — likely a minimum-package floor, not a typical ticket. Verify with Rodrigo.

sources.diff: Indexed 2026 monthly tasting conversion rate summary (Jan-Apr, 124 attended / 49 won / 29 pending / 39.5% YTD) + Oct 26 tasting attendee detail (6 attendees, 2 won, 1 maybe, 3 lost — 2 of 3 losses cite food/service quality). Per-owner signal captured for Andre / Ren / Eduarda. Data quality issue flagged on row 5 of Oct 26 sheet.
